floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: (s32) mask cast is inert-likely (SImode const canonicalization makes all three spellings identical RTL); the s16 tmp clamp is load-bearing-LIKELY via constant materialization (targe

Applied the Judge's mandated clean rewrite (plain g_file_vram_timer assignment, ~0x10001 mask) -> honest clean floor is 1, not 0; the 0-floor required the exact typed-literal-holder construct the Judg
Structural session closed both open frontier leads (file-scope const, arithmetic/enum RHS respellings) — all score 1, same forbidden-mechanism boundary as s2; floor unchanged at 1

Permuter (337-iter random campaign, honest single-func workspace) independently rediscovered the SAME forbidden typed-literal-holder cheat s2/s3 already killed by hand -- no legitimate closing lever e

frontier.hypothesis: A structurally different chassis (varying the WHOLE function's control-flow shape rather than just the clamp-store RHS) could give the permuter a genuinely new basin to search.
frontier.mechanism: unclear -- s3 already measured that the one structural variant tried (else-if merge of the two clamp checks) regresses the score (11 vs 1), and the residual itself is a single-instruction constant-materialization encoding choice (ori vs addiu) with no surrounding structure that plausibly varies it without touching unrelated, already-verified-correct code.
frontier.next_probe: If a future session wants to spend more permuter time here, the only untried angle is reseeding with PERM_LINESWAP/PERM_GENERAL directed at the trailing v>>4 rounding block (in case some reordering there changes constant-pool/scheduling context for the earlier store) -- low expected yield given s3's structural finding, but it is the one untried chassis.

frontier.hypothesis: Re-verify (per the open frontier item carried since s2/s3) whether the trailing block's `v` local could ever legitimately be hoisted to also carry -7168 for a second, real purpose.
frontier.mechanism: s3 already closed this analytically: target asm ordering requires g_file_vram_timer's read (for v) to happen AFTER the clamp store, so no natural reshuffle lets v hold the pre-clamp constant without inventing an artificial second purpose.
frontier.next_probe: Effectively exhausted; a future session could still try a permuter PERM_LINESWAP sweep over statement orderings just to have a fully mechanical negative, but confidence this yields anything is very low given s3's ordering-constraint analysis.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Two codegen-control constructs fail the human-programmer test (test #2) and the semantic-purpose test (test #1): (1) a typed s16 intermediate used to clamp a u16 global — `{ s16 tmp = -0x1C00; g_file_vram_timer = tmp; }` — has no semantic difference from `g_file_vram_timer = -0x1C00;` and the SOTN named-intermediate sanction does not cover typed-literal coercion; (2) an explicit `(s32)` cast on a 32-bit AND mask — `& (s32)0xFFFEFFFE` — changes the RTL type of the expression (SI vs USI) without changing the MIPS `and` instruction or the runtime result, and a human programmer would write `& ~0x10001` or the unsigned literal without a signed cast. Both constructs change how GCC 2.7.2 sees the type of a computation in order to influence scheduling or RTL analysis, with no observable semantic effect. Next action: a worker must produce a clean pure-C form — replace `{ s16 tmp = -0x1C00; g_file_vram_timer = tmp; }` with `g_file_vram_timer = -0x1C00;` (or the natural u16 equivalent) and `& (s32)0xFFFEFFFE` with `& ~0x10001` (or `& 0xFFFEFFFE` without cast), then verify sandbox distance is 0 and SHA1 == oracle before re-submitting.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)

Match must land with a plain assignment to `g_file_vram_timer` (u16) — no `{ s16 tmp = …; }` local, no `*(s16 *)&…` pointer-cast pun, no other typed-lvalue coercion whose sole function is to change the HImode-vs-SImode store encoding. The `& ~0x10001;` mask form is accepted.
